questions.question: Which color is most associated with creature destruction and removal?
questions.explanation: Black holds the largest share of unconditional creature removal in MTG's color pie.

questions.question: Which of these is a classic one-mana red burn spell that deals 3 damage?
questions.explanation: Lightning Bolt: 1R, deals 3 damage to any target. The benchmark every burn spell is measured against.

questions.question: Which card is shown here?

question_image.alt_text: A famous artifact card from Alpha

questions.explanation: Black Lotus — Alpha (lea), 1993. Sacrifice it for three mana of any one color. The most iconic card in Magic's history.

questions.question: Which planeswalker first appeared on the card 'Jace, the Mind Sculptor'?
questions.explanation: Jace, the Mind Sculptor debuted in Worldwake (2010) and is widely considered one of the most powerful planeswalkers ever printed.

questions.question: Which of these is part of the original Power Nine?
questions.explanation: The Power Nine: Black Lotus, the five Moxen, Time Walk, Ancestral Recall, and Timetwister — all from Alpha/Beta/Unlimited.

questions.question: Counterspell's original mana cost is...
questions.explanation: Counterspell costs UU — two blue mana, no generic. It's been the gold standard for counter magic since Alpha.

questions.question: Which creature type is associated with the card 'Llanowar Elves'?
questions.explanation: Llanowar Elves is the iconic one-mana 1/1 Elf Druid that taps for green — printed in nearly every era of Magic.

questions.question: Which keyword ability gives a creature 'this creature can't be blocked except by creatures with flying or reach'?
questions.explanation: Flying — one of the original evergreen abilities. Reach was introduced as the counter-keyword so ground creatures could block fliers.

questions.question: What's the mana cost of the card shown here? (Name and mana cost are hidden!)
questions.explanation: Lightning Bolt costs R — one red mana, deals 3 damage to any target. The benchmark every burn spell is measured against.

questions.question: Guess this card by its art and text alone — the name is hidden.
questions.explanation: Counterspell — UU, counter target spell. Since Alpha, still the cleanest counter in the game.

questions.question: What rarity does this card carry?
questions.explanation: Counterspell has historically been Common or Uncommon depending on the set — it's a curriculum staple, not a chase card.

questions.question: Roughly how much does this card cost on the Scryfall market?
questions.explanation: Force of Will sits in the $50–$80 range on most Scryfall-tracked printings — a tier-1 eternal staple but not as eye-watering as the Reserved-List Power Nine.
